prompt:
A compact 200 Wh portable battery pack sits on an outdoor table, connected by neatly routed cables to a single fold-out solar panel aimed toward the sun. The battery pack has a simple display panel glowing softly to show charge level as it cycles, with DC and USB ports clearly visible but without any brand logos or readable text. A small notepad with hand-drawn charge/discharge arrows, along with a multimeter or clamp meter resting nearby, makes it clear that the user is deliberately charging and discharging the pack to keep it healthy as part of routine maintenance.

The setting is a bright near-future solarpunk balcony or small patio, with railings, potted plants, and a distant hint of rooftop solar arrays in soft focus. Lighting is natural and cinematic, emphasizing the blue sky reflections on the panel and the subtle indicators on the battery pack in a photorealistic 512×512 composition. The scene feels calm and organized, with no clutter, no visible brand names, and no full human figures—at most, a forearm or hand adjusting a cable—to highlight the idea of cycling a modest solar-powered battery pack for long-term reliability.
